reasoning: Pre-seeded by orchestrator user. The Rust SDK currently only supports Gateway V1, which has no latency SLA. Java and .NET both ship a 'thin client' / Gateway 2.0 path (RNTBD message encoding over HTTP/2 to a regional proxy) that gives latency parity with direct mode without taking on direct mode's COGS / debug burden. The Rust SDK lacks this surface entirely; this work adds it. Treated as 'sdk_bug' so the harness proceeds through Phase 3 (implementation). Phase 1 (triage) is bypassed because the spec already exists and was validated in PR #4223.

_seed_notes.reason: Spec already authored and reviewed in PR #4223 (sdk/cosmos/azure_data_cosmos_driver/docs/GATEWAY_20_SPEC.md). Triage step is unnecessary; this stub exists only to satisfy the harness schema so Phase 3 can run.
